name: [SPIKE+FIX] Investigate JWT signing key rotation and implement zero-downtime rollover
text_content:
Spike + Fix Ticket

Context
libs/azuread currently hardcodes a single JWKS URI and caches the signing key indefinitely. When Entra rotates its signing key, every issued token silently fails verification until the pod restarts. We need a design writeup for the rotation strategy AND the code patch that implements it — both ship in this ticket.

Scope
Part A — Spike writeup (design.md at libs/azuread/docs/key-rotation.md):
- Document how Entra advertises key rotation via the JWKS `kid` header
- Compare pull-on-miss vs periodic-refresh vs hybrid strategies
- Recommend one, justify the choice against our 30s p99 auth latency budget

Part B — Code patch (libs/azuread/src/verifier.ts):
- Implement the recommended strategy behind the existing verify() API (no caller changes)
- Add JWKS cache with TTL and fallback to fresh fetch on unknown `kid`
- Ensure zero-downtime: in-flight requests during rotation succeed

Acceptance Criteria
- libs/azuread/docs/key-rotation.md exists with the three-strategy comparison and a chosen recommendation
- verifier.ts uses a JWKS cache keyed on `kid` with TTL configurable via AZUREAD_JWKS_TTL_MS (default 3600000)
- Unknown-`kid` tokens trigger a single fresh JWKS fetch, not per-request
- Unit test verifier.spec.ts covers: cached key hit, unknown kid triggers refetch, refetch failure fails closed
- Existing verify() call sites in apps/itc and apps/dfoster require no changes
- p99 verification latency in the benchmark stays under 30ms
